rulings.Case Title: MORVEN LIBURD VS. BOSA DEVELOPMENT CALIFORNIA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Depo Attendance And Testimony
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday February 14, 2012 Line 1 - Urata & Sons' Cement,Inc.'S Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance And Testimony Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Urata & Sons' Cement, Inc.'s motion to compel deposition attendance and testimony is granted in part and denied in part. Bosa Development California II, Inc. is ordered to produce its agent or agents most qualified to testify and to produce documents concerning the topics in Urata & Sons Cement, Inc.'s November 29, 2011 Notice of Taking Deposition of Bosa Development California II, Inc.'s Persons Most Qualified no later than March 1, 2012. The deposition shall continue day-to-day until completed. Urata & Sons' motion asked for relief solely in terms of the scheduling of the deposition. Therefore, this order does not purport to rule on any of Bosa's substantive or privilege objections to the document request contained in the notice of deposition.

rulings.Case Title: MORVEN LIBURD VS. BOSA DEVELOPMENT CALIFORNIA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendant Bosa Development California Ii, Inc. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Urata & Sons' Cement,Inc.'S Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance And Testimony - part II The court's order of January 12, 2012 (the Hon. Harold Kahn, presiding) severed the personal injury and insurance fraud actions; vacated the April 12, 2012 trial date for those matters; set a new trial date of September 4, 2012, and re-set all pretrial deadlines to be governed by the September 4, 2012 continued trial date. Specifically, with respect to discovery, the court ordered that "the parties shall meet and confer to minimize the inconvenience / cost to anyone involved in only one aspect of either case."Counsel for Urata & Sons represents that information to be obtained from the deposition of Bosa's PMK is necessary for the mediation scheduled for March 6, 2012. Bosa Development California filed a cross-complaint against Aon Reed Stenhouse, Inc. and Aon Risk Services West Inc. (collectively "Aon") on January 23, 2011. Counsel for Bosa represents that Aon's counsel has advised Bosa's counsel that Aon will respond to Bosa's Cross-Complaint by March 9, 2012. However, the declaration does not state when AON was served, or whether Bosa and Aon have stipulated to a 15-day extension of time to respond. The March 1st deposition date should give AON the opportunity to appear in the action and attend the deposition in advance of the mediation date should it wish to do so, while allowing Urata & Sons to obtain information it asserts is necessary for a productive mediation.The request for monetary sanctions is denied.Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: MORVEN LIBURD VS. BOSA DEVELOPMENT CALIFORNIA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Urata & Sons' Cement,Inc.'S Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance And Testimony
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday February 14, 2012 Line 1 - Cross defendant Bosa Development's Motion for a protective order. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Cross-Defendant Bosa Development's motion for a protective order is denied. Bosa Development California II, Inc. is ordered to produce its agent or agents most qualified to testify and to produce documents concerning the topics in Urata & Sons Cement, Inc.'s November 29, 2011 Notice of Taking Deposition of Bosa Development California II, Inc.'s Persons Most Qualified no later than March 1, 2012. The deposition shall continue day-to-day until completed. Urata & Sons' motion asked for relief solely in terms of the scheduling of the deposition. Therefore, this order does not purport to rule on any of Bosa's substantive or privilege objections to the document request contained in the notice of deposition.

rulings.Case Title: MORVEN LIBURD VS. BOSA DEVELOPMENT CALIFORNIA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendant Bosa Development'S Motion For A Protective Order.
rulings.Rulings: Cross Defendant Bosa Development'S Motion For A Protective Order, - Part II Cross defendant Bosa Development's Motion for a protective order. The court's order of January 12, 2012 (the Hon. Harold Kahn, presiding) severed the personal injury and insurance fraud actions; vacated the April 12, 2012 trial date for those matters; set a new trial date of September 4, 2012, and re-set all pretrial deadlines to be governed by the September 4, 2012 continued trial date. Specifically, with respect to discovery, the court ordered that "the parties shall meet and confer to minimize the inconvenience / cost to anyone involved in only one aspect of either case." Counsel for Urata & Sons represents that information to be obtained from the deposition of Bosa's PMK is necessary for the mediation scheduled for March 6, 2012. Bosa Development California filed a cross-complaint against Aon Reed Stenhouse, Inc. and Aon Risk Services West Inc. (collectively "Aon") on January 23, 2011. Counsel for Bosa represents that Aon's counsel has advised Bosa's counsel that Aon will respond to Bosa's Cross-Complaint by March 9, 2012. However, the declaration does not state when AON was served, or whether Bosa and Aon have stipulated to a 15-day extension of time to respond. The March 1st deposition deadline should give AON the opportunity to appear in the action and attend the deposition in advance of the mediation date should it wish to do so, while allowing Urata & Sons to obtain information it asserts is necessary for a productive mediation. The request for monetary sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: LOUIS GAMACHE et al VS. NICK VOLENTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 14, 2012, Line 8. DEFENDANT PRIME PACIFIC INVESTMENTS, INC. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action 1, 3, 5, 6, 9, 11, 13, and 14. These claims are to be asserted against the landlords and/or owners and Plaintiff concedes that defendant does not fall into those categories. Sustained without leave to amend as to cause of action 7 because it is not an independent claim and it is repetitive of the negligence cause of action. Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to causes of action 2, 4, 8, 10, and 12 to plead facts to support these claims as to this defendant. The Court rejects defendant's arguments regarding joinder and the insufficiency of the class allegations. rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO ADVISORS, LLC SUCCESSOR TO WACHOVIA VS. STIFEL NICOLAUS, LLC ("STIFEL") et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 14, 2012, Line 15. DEFENDANT STIFEL, NICOLAUS & CO., INC. MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES. Granted in full. Respondent is entitled to its appellate fees and the fees for bringing this motion pursuant to Civil Code section 3426.4 because petitioner commenced its UTSA action in bad faith. There is no basis to apportion the appellate fees, especially given that the finder of fact concluded that the claims were so interrelated that apportionment was not feasible. The arbitrators and trial court never made any prospective rulings regarding appellate fees and Petitioner reads too much into the arbitration award and order confirming the award. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO ADVISORS, LLC SUCCESSOR TO WACHOVIA VS. STIFEL NICOLAUS, LLC ("STIFEL") et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Atty'S Fees On Appeal
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 14, 2012, Line 15. RESPONDENT MICHAEL NIELSEN Mtn For Atty'S Fees On Appeal. Granted in full. Respondent is entitled to his appellate fees and the fees for bringing this motion pursuant to Civil Code section 3426.4 because petitioner commenced its UTSA action in bad faith. There is no basis to apportion the appellate fees, especially given that the finder of fact concluded that the claims were so interrelated that apportionment was not feasible. The arbitrators and trial court never made any prospective rulings regarding appellate fees and Petitioner reads too much into the arbitration award and order confirming the award. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CAI XIA ZHANG VS. PETER H. BRESLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 14, 2012, Line 22. DEFENDANT PETER BRESLER, FAYE LEE DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the first claim. Plaintiff sufficiently pleads liability based on defendant's negligent preparation of the 2007 will and trust. Sustained without leave to amend as to the second claim. Defendant did not owe a duty of care to plaintiff with regard to the proposed amendment. Liability only arises after the testator executes the documents.
